_meta.description: Standalone events placed in rooms — environmental atmosphere, interactive mechanics, and one-time discoveries. Referenced by rooms.json via possible_events.

fields.name: Display name
fields.tier: 1–5 — matches room tier range where this event is appropriate
fields.on_notice: What the player sees that alerts them to the event's presence
fields.on_engage: Full description when the player interacts with the event
fields.on_resolve: Text after the event is completed or passes
fields.options: For interactive events — the choices available and their outcomes
fields.repeatable: Whether this event can appear again on re-entry

events.name: Distant Sound
events.on_notice: Something echoes from another part of the dungeon — too far away to locate, close enough to hear.
events.on_resolve: The sound does not repeat. Whatever made it either stopped or moved.
variants: A scream from somewhere below. Sharp, short, then nothing.
Stone grinding on stone — slow, deliberate, as if a very large door is moving.
Laughter, low and without humor, from a direction you can't fix.
A rhythmic impact, like something being hammered, that stops mid-sequence.
A voice — unintelligible at this distance — that rises and then drops below hearing.

events.name: Torch Extinction
events.on_notice: The light changes — specifically, it diminishes. One of the wall torches is guttering.
events.on_engage: The torch dies. The room goes to whatever ambient light remains, which may be very little or none.
events.on_resolve: You adjust to the dark or produce another light source.

options.description: Light a new torch from inventory.
Attempt to relight the wall torch.
options.outcome: Wall torch relights. Burns for 3 more rooms.
All Perception and combat checks at -3 until light is restored. Trap detection becomes nearly impossible.

events.name: Haunted Whispers
events.on_notice: The walls are doing something. Not a sound exactly — more of an intention that presents as sound. Words are almost there.
events.on_engage: You stop and listen. The words clarify — not fully, not enough to transcribe, but enough to understand that they are about you. Not a warning. Not an attack. An assessment. Something is taking stock of you.
events.on_resolve: The whispers stop at the room boundary. Whatever assessed you made its judgment and lost interest.

options.description: Stand still and try to make out the words.
options.on_success: You catch a phrase — specific, relevant: the location of a hidden room, the weakness of a nearby enemy, or the content of the next locked chest. This is true information.
options.on_fail: The words fragment into noise. Willpower check DC 10 or gain 'unsettled' status: -1 to all checks for 2 rooms.

options.description: Keep moving. Don't engage with whatever this is.
options.outcome: No effect. The whispers follow for one room, then stop.

events.name: Hidden Draft
events.on_notice: A draft from a featureless wall — cold, moving air from a place where walls do not move air.
events.on_engage: You press along the wall. The stone gives slightly at one point — not crumbling, but pivoting. The mechanism is old and smooth from use. This wall has been opened before, regularly, by someone who knew exactly where to push.
events.on_resolve: The hidden room is revealed on the map. Its entrance is marked for future use.

options.description: Investigate the draft and find the door.
options.on_success: Hidden room discovered. Added to map.
options.on_fail: Nothing found this pass. Can try again with +2 on the next attempt.

events.name: Wall Inscription
events.on_notice: Text on the wall. Not a rune — a language, written with effort, at a specific height. Someone stood here and wrote this deliberately.
events.on_engage: You read it. The language is old but not entirely foreign.
events.on_resolve: The inscription remains. You've understood what it wanted to say.

inscription_pool.text: The vault is sealed against the crown. This was intentional. The king does not know who insisted on it.
Guard rotation: crypt, armory, upper hall, armory, crypt. The crypt guard does not respond to sound. Only movement.
Do not look at the throne room carvings for more than a moment. This is not superstition.
The well is not connected to the water table. It is connected to something else. Splash says enough.
Year 4 of the binding. The king has stopped speaking. He gives commands in writing now. The writing changes every week. Less each time.
The court mage left by a door I have never found. I have been looking since the binding. If you find it, note that it goes up, not down.
The slimes in the lower hall are afraid of fire. Use this.
The Crypt Warden is a person. Was a person. His name was Aldric. He would not have wanted this. Remember that if you face him.

events.name: Well of Fate
events.on_notice: The well is glowing. Not strongly — a blue-white light rising from below, like moonlight reflected in water, except there is no moonlight here. The DO NOT DRINK inscription is still there. The glow is recent.
events.on_engage: You look down into the well. The glow comes from far below — too far to be a floor. The light pulses slowly. The well is waiting for something, in the patient way of things that have been waiting a very long time.
events.on_resolve: The well's light dims slowly after the exchange. Whatever it wanted from the transaction, it received.

options.description: Drop a coin into the well.
options.on_engage: The coin falls. You count three seconds before you hear it hit. The light pulses once, strongly. Then something happens.

results.description: A pale shape rises from the well. The coin displeased it, or pleased it in the wrong way.
A chill settles in the chest that doesn't leave. Max HP reduced by 5 until rested at a shrine.
The floor drops away briefly and you are somewhere else.
The water shows you something briefly — a map of the dungeon's current floor, landmarks included, for ten seconds.
Warmth from below. You feel completely, immediately well.
The light settles into you. All stats +2 for five rooms.
The coin comes back up. With it, a ring — carried on the surface of the water. You didn't put it there.

options.description: Drink from the well despite the warning.
options.on_engage: The water is cold. It tastes of copper and something floral. It is wrong in a way that your body identifies immediately.

results.description: Whatever is in the water is not for the living. Severe poison: 8 damage per turn, 4 turns.
The dungeon looks different for three rooms — enemies appear where there are none, doorways appear in solid walls. Perception checks at -4.
Your body processes the water. You feel extraordinary. Maybe the warning was for lesser constitutions.

options.outcome: Nothing happens. The light continues to pulse.

events.name: Shrine Blessing
events.on_notice: The altar is warm in a room that has no heat source. The warmth is specific and directional — it comes from the stone itself.
events.on_engage: You approach the altar. The warmth increases. The figure above it — robed, faceless, arms extended — is unchanged, but the attitude of it reads differently up close. Not threatening. Waiting.
events.on_resolve: The warmth settles. The altar cools slightly, its purpose served for now.

options.description: Lay your hand on the altar and pray — to whatever this is.

additional.description: The warmth moves to the cursed item and holds there. Its effect is quiet for now.

options.on_engage: The stone under your hand is very warm, then hot, then warm again. You feel better in a way that is not reducible to HP. Something in the dungeon has noticed you, briefly, and chosen to be kind about it.

options.description: Leave an item as an offering.
options.on_engage: You place the item on the altar. It is accepted — the item recedes into the stone, slowly, as if absorbed. The altar hums.

outcome.description: The offering is taken. In its place, something better is left on the altar.

options.outcome: The warmth doesn't follow you, but it remains available. You can come back.

events.name: Active Alchemy Table
events.on_notice: Among the broken glass, one station is intact — burner lit, flask in place, reagents still accessible. Someone stopped mid-work.
events.on_engage: The setup is: a sealed flask of stabilized compound over a flame, with two unused reagent slots. The notebook is open to a recipe. You can read it. Whether you can follow it is another question.
events.on_resolve: The station cools. The burner gutters out after use.

options.description: Follow the recipe in the notebook.
options.on_success: The compound combines correctly. Result: one health_potion_standard created.
options.on_fail: The compound does something unexpected — not explosive, but wrong. Flask must be discarded. No result.

options.description: Add materials from your inventory to the active flask.

on_success.description: The improvisation works. Result is random based on material used.

options.on_fail: Explosion. 10 fire damage to the player. Station destroyed.

options.description: Take the three sealed urns without opening them.
options.outcome: Three sealed urns added to inventory. Each is a volatile flask — use as bomb_fire, but with additional random effect on detonation.

events.name: The Ritual Circle
events.on_notice: The central white stone calls attention by contrast — everything else in this room is dark and complex. The white is deliberate, prepared.
events.on_engage: You step into the circle. The silver wire in the floor brightens. The candles flare. The standing stones warm simultaneously. Something is activating — you are the key that was missing.
events.on_resolve: The circle quiets after the activation. The white stone is no longer white — it is the same color as the rest of the floor now. Used.

options.description: Stand in the central white stone and remain.

on_success.description: The ritual completes. You receive a vision: the dungeon's full map revealed, all enemy locations marked for the next 3 rooms, and the knowledge that the Undead King's crown is the seat of his binding.
on_success.bonus: Quest 'The Undead King' advances — 'Weakness of the Crown' objective marked complete.

on_fail.description: The circle's energy is too much. It discharges through you. 20 necrotic damage, and you are ejected from the circle.

options.description: Break the silver wire to disrupt the circle before it activates.
options.on_success: The circle goes dark. No activation, no risk. The room becomes simply an octagonal room.
options.on_fail: You break the wrong part first. The circle activates with you in it — forced to make the Willpower check.

events.name: Ground Tremor
events.on_notice: The floor vibrates — briefly, deeply, as if something very large has moved far below.
events.on_engage: The tremor lasts three seconds. Dust falls from the ceiling. An unsecured torch bracket rotates and the torch falls to the floor, still lit.
events.on_resolve: The dungeon settles. The dust hangs in the air for a minute before dispersing.

outcomes.description: No structural consequence. The dungeon holds.
All fragile items (potions, scrolls) in inventory require a Dexterity check DC 10 or one is broken.
A crack opens in the wall, exposing a cavity. 50% chance of containing a random loot_floor_tier2 item.
A stone falls from the ceiling. Agility DC 11 to avoid, 8 damage on hit.

events.name: The Water Moves
events.on_notice: The water in the flooded hall moves with purpose. Not a current — a displacement.
events.on_engage: Something under the surface is large enough to push water ahead of it. It circles the hall at speed. You cannot see it — the water is too dark.
events.on_resolve: The displacement slows. The thing, whatever it is, submerges fully and does not surface again this pass.

options.on_success: You cross before whatever is in the water decides to engage.
options.on_fail: Something grabs your leg. Strength DC 13 to break free. On failure: pulled under, 15 damage, surfaced on far side.

options.description: Throw something into the far end of the hall to draw the creature away.
options.outcome: The displacement moves toward the sound. Safe crossing window: 2 turns.
